2605.15040.title: Orchard: An Open-Source Agentic Modeling Framework
2605.15040.paper_summary: Agentic modeling aims to transform LLMs into autonomous agents capable of solving complex tasks through planning, reasoning, tool use, and multi-turn interaction with environments. Despite major investment, open research remains constrained by infrastructure and training gaps. Many high-performing systems rely on proprietary codebases, models, or services, while most open-source frameworks focus on orchestration and evaluation rather than scalable agent training. We present Orchard, an open-source framework for scalable agentic modeling. At its core is Orchard Env, a lightweight environment service providing reusable primitives for sandbox lifecycle management across task domains, agent harnesses, and pipeline stages. On top of Orchard Env, we build three agentic modeling recipes. Orchard-SWE targets coding agents. We distill 107K trajectories from MiniMax-M2.5 and Qwen3.5-397B, introduce credit-assignment SFT to learn from productive segments of unresolved trajectories, and apply Balanced Adaptive Rollout for RL. Starting from Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking, Orchard-SWE achieves 64.3% on SWE-bench Verified after SFT and 67.5% after SFT+RL, setting a new state of the art among open-source models of comparable size. Orchard-GUI trains a 4B vision-language computer-use agent using only 0.4K distilled trajectories and 2.2K open-ended tasks. It achieves 74.1%, 67.0%, and 64.0% success rates on WebVoyager, Online-Mind2Web, and DeepShop, respectively, making it the strongest open-source model while remaining competitive with proprietary systems. Orchard-Claw targets personal assistant agents. Trained with only 0.2K synthetic tasks, it achieves 59.6% pass@3 on Claw-Eval and 73.9% when paired with a stronger ZeroClaw harness. Collectively, these results show that a lightweight, open, harness-agnostic environment layer enables reusable agentic data, training recipes, and evaluations across domains.
2605.15040.paper_summary_zh: 代理建模旨在將大型語言模型（LLMs）轉變為能夠通過規劃、推理、工具使用和與環境的多輪互動來解決複雜任務的自主代理。儘管投入了大量資金，開放研究仍然受到基礎設施和訓練差距的限制。許多高性能系統依賴於專有的代碼庫、模型或服務，而大多數開源框架則專注於協調和評估，而非可擴展的代理訓練。我們提出了 Orchard，一個可擴展的代理建模開源框架。其核心是 Orchard Env，一個輕量級的環境服務，提供可重用的原語，用於跨任務領域、代理工具和管道階段的沙盒生命週期管理。在 Orchard Env 之上，我們構建了三個代理建模配方。Orchard-SWE 針對編碼代理。我們從 MiniMax-M2.5 和 Qwen3.5-397B 中提取了 107K 軌跡，引入了信用分配 SFT 以從未解決軌跡的有效段落中學習，並應用平衡自適應回報以進行強化學習。從 Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking 開始，Orchard-SWE 在 SFT 後在 SWE-bench Verified 上達到 64.3%，在 SFT+RL 後達到 67.5%，在可比大小的開源模型中設立了新的最先進水平。Orchard-GUI 使用僅 0.4K 提煉的軌跡和 2.2K 開放式任務訓練一個 4B 視覺-語言計算機使用代理。它在 WebVoyager、Online-Mind2Web 和 DeepShop 上分別達到 74.1%、67.0% 和 64.0% 的成功率，使其成為最強的開源模型，同時與專有系統保持競爭力。Orchard-Claw 針對個人助理代理。僅用 0.2K 合成任務進行訓練，它在 Claw-Eval 上達到 59.6% 的 pass@3，與更強的 ZeroClaw 工具配對時達到 73.9%。總體而言，這些結果顯示，輕量級、開放、無工具依賴的環境層使得可重用的代理數據、訓練配方和跨領域的評估成為可能。
2605.15040.author: Baolin Peng et.al.
2605.15040.authors: Baolin Peng, Wenlin Yao, Qianhui Wu, Hao Cheng, Xiao Yu, Rui Yang, Tao Ge, Alessandrio Sordoni, Xingdi Yuan, Yelong Shen, Pengcheng He, Tong Zhang, Zhou Yu, Jianfeng Gao
